title: Baseball

jokes.text: It's food-nomenal. Really. It really is.
jokes.setup: Discussing Chick-fil-A narrative
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'food' and 'phenomenal'
jokes.explanation: Simple pun but executed with earnestness that makes it funnier

jokes.text: My bladder was like a fuckin' blimp.
jokes.setup: Stuck in traffic for two hours due to construction
jokes.punchline: Graphic comparison of full bladder to a blimp
jokes.explanation: Vivid, crude imagery that's both relatable and absurd

jokes.text: If you ever want me to confess, don't rip out my fingernails, just make me eat like a vegan for a fuckin' week.
jokes.setup: Context of enduring terrible morning at health food event
jokes.punchline: Vegan diet as torture worse than fingernail removal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina voice - extreme, specific, and character-revealing dark humor

jokes.text: The VP look like she's about ready to open up a vein.
jokes.setup: Selina looking miserable at health food event
jokes.punchline: Suicide metaphor for her visible despair
jokes.explanation: Dark but economical observation that captures the VP's misery

jokes.text: Yeah, I know, feel my soul slide out of my ass.
jokes.setup: Being told she should go schmooze more
jokes.punchline: Visceral, disgusting metaphor for soul-crushing nature of the job
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disgusting and specific imagery that's pure Selina

jokes.text: This smells like the inside of a taxi cab.
jokes.setup: Reacting to some food item
jokes.punchline: Specific, unappetizing comparison
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective specific comparison that's relatable

jokes.text: Those yellow things look... so...
jokes.setup: Trying to make small talk about healthy food
jokes.punchline: Complete inability to finish positive thought about food
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Selina's complete discomfort

jokes.text: Orgasmic's more like it, right?
jokes.setup: VP calling food 'organic'
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual reference to the Vice President
jokes.explanation: Awkward sexual innuendo that creates uncomfortable moment

jokes.text: I'll have some of what you're having.
jokes.setup: After vendor's 'orgasmic' comment
jokes.punchline: Selina's dry, understated response to sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that deflects awkwardness with wit

jokes.text: I meant for him.
jokes.setup: Someone saying 'that's gotta be tough' about Gary's jock father
jokes.punchline: Gary clarifying he meant it's tough for his father, not him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - reveals his oblivious self-confidence in three words

jokes.text: Nobody needs to know which of my eyebrow hairs needs to be plucked.
jokes.setup: Ken getting too close with camera
jokes.punchline: Specific, vain concern about appearance on camera
jokes.explanation: Character-specific vanity that's relatable but petty

jokes.text: Turn that frown... upside-down.
jokes.setup: Trying to cheer up visibly miserable VP
jokes.punchline: Painfully awkward pause mid-cliché
jokes.explanation: Awkward timing creates cringe comedy from tired phrase

jokes.text: It's his feely, little, touchy, hot hands.
jokes.setup: Complaining about vendor touching her
jokes.punchline: String of adjectives building disgust
jokes.explanation: Building revulsion through accumulating descriptors

jokes.text: Someone needs to tell him that he's working with the White House and not 'Penthouse.'
jokes.setup: Vendor being inappropriately touchy
jokes.punchline: White House/Penthouse magazine comparison
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that makes her point about inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: You stole a car?
jokes.setup: Amy saying 'things got a little bit out of hand'
jokes.punchline: Selina's assumption jumps to car theft instead of sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection showing Selina's mind works in unexpected ways

jokes.text: I could probably have unprotected sex with 30 men, one right after the other, and it would be totally fine.
jokes.setup: Discussing her fertility
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and graphic hypothetical scenario
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that reveals Selina's complete lack of filter

jokes.text: I mean, no, it wouldn't be totally fine. I mean, that would be a horrible thing.
jokes.setup: Her own outrageous statement about 30 men
jokes.punchline: Immediate backtracking and clarification
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina - says something outrageous then tries to walk it back

jokes.text: How do you think that plays?
jokes.setup: Discussing unwed VP mother one heartbeat from presidency
jokes.punchline: Dry political understatement about the optics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy response - politically astute and understated

jokes.text: Gary's bag demonstration - 60 separate pockets within the area
jokes.setup: Gary showing off his organizational system
jokes.punchline: Absurdly over-organized bag system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary character beat - obsessive organization as comedy

jokes.text: Scented, unscented? Scented. Bam!
jokes.setup: Gary demonstrating his bag organization skills
jokes.punchline: Getting wipes correct and celebrating dramatically
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Gary's pride in menial tasks

jokes.text: Unless we go on some sort of special vacation to Mexico and... kill the doctor in the desert.
jokes.setup: Discussing abortion options
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of murdering the doctor
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark escalation delivered casually - pure Veep darkness

jokes.text: You take hundreds of pictures of herons catching fish? Because otherwise you don't need a 1D.
jokes.setup: Discussing camera equipment
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific scenario for expensive camera use
jokes.explanation: Ken's specific photography knowledge creates unexpected humor

jokes.text: Photos with the nutritionists, hang with the players, over to the school, then we pregnancy test you to fuck.
jokes.setup: Listing day's activities
jokes.punchline: Pregnancy testing as final agenda item
jokes.explanation: Amy's blunt efficiency applied to personal crisis

jokes.text: How's the weather up there in your asshole?
jokes.setup: Jonah greeting Mike
jokes.punchline: Insult playing on height/head-up-ass metaphors
jokes.explanation: Classic Mike-Jonah antagonism with creative insult

jokes.text: One day you're gonna wake up and you're gonna find your lungs have turned to pastry.
jokes.setup: Mike smoking
jokes.punchline: Absurd specific health consequence
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd health warning

jokes.text: The best thing for her legacy is if she's assassinated before she starts showing.
jokes.setup: Learning Selina might be pregnant
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting assassination as PR solution
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark political calculation delivered matter-of-factly - brilliant Veep darkness

jokes.text: Business card bukkake.
jokes.setup: Describing networking at stadium
jokes.punchline: Sexual metaphor for business card exchange
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crude Dan metaphor that's both shocking and economical

jokes.text: Razor blades, a large amount of pain killers.
jokes.setup: Amy asking what she should get while they're at the store
jokes.punchline: Suicide supplies as shopping list
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Amy's career prospects

jokes.text: Amy, come on, career-wise, that's like joining scientology or getting a fucking neck tattoo.
jokes.setup: Amy being pregnant
jokes.punchline: Comparing pregnancy to career-killing choices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan - brutal honesty with specific, escalating comparisons

jokes.text: My first thought was, 'there goes your figure,' but I didn't say that because I thought it would be upsetting.
jokes.setup: Claiming his first thought wasn't about Amy's replacement
jokes.punchline: Reveals even more insensitive first thought while claiming sensitivity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan moment - reveals his awfulness while pretending to be considerate

jokes.text: Thanks. You know, if it's a boy, maybe I'll name him after you. Call him fuck weasel.
jokes.setup: Dan's insensitive comments
jokes.punchline: Naming baby 'fuck weasel' after Dan
jokes.explanation: Amy's sharp comeback using Dan's own language against him

jokes.text: Amy's practically having a baby about it.
jokes.setup: Introducing Amy while pregnancy is secret concern
jokes.punchline: Accidental pregnancy pun in innocent context
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - innocent phrase becomes loaded given context

jokes.text: Are you networking with an eight-year-old?
jokes.setup: Dan asking kid about his father's job connections
jokes.punchline: Calling out Dan's inappropriate networking
jokes.explanation: Perfect observation of Dan's shameless networking instincts

jokes.text: 'Lion King' DVD and two shots of ritalin.
jokes.setup: How to keep kids entertained for an hour
jokes.punchline: Child medication as crowd control
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medicating children for convenience

jokes.text: That's how I was raised.
jokes.setup: Amy saying he'd be a great dad after ritalin comment
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of medicated childhood
jokes.explanation: Reveals Dan's background while explaining his personality

jokes.text: I'd like to free that boy from the prison of obesity.
jokes.setup: Briefing on health initiative origins
jokes.punchline: Dramatic metaphor about fat kid
jokes.explanation: Absurdly dramatic political language applied to child obesity

jokes.text: I believe he was reassigned because of his cologne. It was Paco Rabanne.
jokes.setup: Question about firing Secret Service agent for smiling
jokes.punchline: Deflection to specific cologne brand
jokes.explanation: Absurd deflection with specific brand reference makes it funnier

jokes.text: That's like carbon offsetting.
jokes.setup: Giving boring answer to boring question
jokes.punchline: Comparing his response strategy to environmental concept
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on his own press strategy using unexpected metaphor

jokes.text: I spent a night in jail when I was 19 once. That was more fun than this.
jokes.setup: Being trapped at the stadium
jokes.punchline: Comparing current situation unfavorably to jail
jokes.explanation: Reveals character background while emphasizing her misery

jokes.text: I'm gonna kill you and eat you, because I will not eat another fuckin' falafel.
jokes.setup: Being trapped with bad food
jokes.punchline: Cannibalism as preferable alternative to falafel
jokes.explanation: Extreme escalation that's both shocking and character-specific

jokes.text: Hey, Jimmy fuckin' Olsen, get lost before I shove that thing up your ass and give you another kind of red eye to worry about.
jokes.setup: Ken bothering her with camera
jokes.punchline: Superman reference plus camera/red eye pun threat
jokes.explanation: Creative insult combining pop culture reference with photography pun

jokes.text: Did you put it in your mouth?
jokes.setup: Amy returning pen cap
jokes.punchline: Mike's germaphobic concern about pen cap
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Mike's neuroses about germs

jokes.text: Holy f... uck.
jokes.setup: Trying to curse while lip-reading Gary
jokes.punchline: Can't complete swear word naturally
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy from trying to hide cursing from lip-reader

jokes.text: I can carry the baby around in a little sling or something like that.
jokes.setup: Learning about possible pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Gary assuming he'd be primary caregiver
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - immediately inserting himself into situation

jokes.text: Is Jonah the dad? Is that why you look like you wanna drink a bottle of drano?
jokes.setup: Seeing Amy's distress about pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Jonah paternity would justify suicide
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Jonah being father, with specific poison reference

jokes.text: They're gonna be pulling that kid out of you in shifts.
jokes.setup: Jonah as potential father
jokes.punchline: Baby would be so large it requires shift work to deliver
jokes.explanation: Vivid, absurd imagery based on Jonah's height

jokes.text: You don't have any idea what you're talking about. Fuckin' idiot.
jokes.setup: Jonah giving wrong baseball information
jokes.punchline: Selina's brutal dismissal of her own advisor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's temper and Jonah's incompetence

jokes.text: I'm with the team... my playas. Boom shaka laka. That's how they talk.
jokes.setup: Gary trying to sound cool on phone with dad
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at sports slang with explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary cringe - trying too hard to impress his father

jokes.text: I do know, however, that the past few weeks she has been pretty busy.
jokes.setup: Pharmacist asking about sister's pregnancy timeline
jokes.punchline: Gary inadvertently implying Selina's been sexually active
jokes.explanation: Gary's innocent comment accidentally sounds suggestive

jokes.text: You proposed by giving me some sort of a ring in a heart-shaped, fuckin' oyster thing or something.
jokes.setup: Creating fake engagement story
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific romantic scenario made up on spot
jokes.explanation: Selina improvising ridiculous romantic details under pressure

jokes.text: I don't know how fucking big my finger is, Ted!
jokes.setup: Ted asking for ring size
jokes.punchline: Angry outburst at reasonable question
jokes.explanation: Selina's stress causing irrational anger at simple request

jokes.text: Read my lips... Don't read my lips.
jokes.setup: Trying to communicate with lip-reading Gary
jokes.punchline: Contradictory instruction about lip reading
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical contradiction that's both funny and functional

jokes.text: Read my lips... Don't read my lips.
jokes.setup: Telling Ken not to reveal information
jokes.punchline: Self-contradictory instruction about lip-reading
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect economy and self-awareness

jokes.text: You having fun? No. Hear that? No fun. You are no fun.
jokes.setup: Dan trying to teach guitar to uninterested kid
jokes.punchline: Child's blunt 'No' deflating Dan's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Child's honesty deflating Dan's attempt at coolness

jokes.text: Or a suicide. I haven't decided which.
jokes.setup: Gary mentioning wedding due to pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Suicide as alternative to wedding
jokes.explanation: Casual mention of suicide as option shows Selina's dark mindset

jokes.text: You think that as a woman I might sense some disturbance in the fuckin' 'lady matrix'?
jokes.setup: Mike asking if she can tell if Selina's pregnant
jokes.punchline: 'Lady matrix' as parody of female intuition trope
jokes.explanation: Brilliant feminist pushback using Matrix reference - clever and pointed

jokes.text: The guy's like gay porn... He's everywhere.
jokes.setup: Leon West showing up unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Comparing journalist to gay porn ubiquity
jokes.explanation: Unexpected comparison that's both crude and accurate

jokes.text: You think that as a woman I might sense some disturbance in the fuckin' 'lady matrix'?
jokes.setup: Dan asking if she can tell if Selina is pregnant
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response using Matrix reference for female intuition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pop culture reference combined with feminist frustration - highly quotable

jokes.text: I said she's repugnant. I said Selina's repugnant.
jokes.setup: Confusion over 'pregnant' vs 'repugnant'
jokes.punchline: Clarification makes it worse - he called her repugnant
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - the 'clarification' makes things much worse

jokes.text: You're very, very pregnant.
jokes.setup: Children singing 'If You're Happy and You Know It'
jokes.punchline: Gary delivering pregnancy test results during children's song
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing of devastating news during innocent children's song

jokes.text: Waited all day for that.
jokes.setup: VP's brief, hurried visit after long wait
jokes.punchline: Child's blunt assessment of anticlimatic experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect button to episode - child's honest disappointment

tmdb_overview: Selina attends a healthy eating event at Camden Yards with some very grumpy fast-food executives, where she also confides a secret to Amy. Mike holds a press briefing for some local news pals, save for the inquisitive Leon West. Later, with the Veep delayed, Amy and Dan are forced to entertain elementary school children.
insight: Political incompetence drives 61 jokes in 33 minutes, but cringe carries more weight than laughs.
